author: potuz
content: It's effectively impossible to do this, at least not that way

author: potuz
content: If we are going to implement a vote like this we need to do it at the duty time level, the VC requests for PTC attestations to sign and it may get many different ones for different keys

author: potuz
content: So the beacon must track on each slot all blocks it's seen

author: potuz
content: And for a given slot when the VC asks it should send a list of expected Payloads, payload statuses and validator IDs that need to sign

author: potuz
content: Otherwise effectively any non finalized root is a possible dependent root

author: 0xunclebill
content: that means a vc would need to query the bn for payload attestation data at every slot. right now i believe most vcs would only query the bn for that data if they have a ptc duty for that slot. the ptc duty endpoint would be effectively useless with this change i think?

author: 0xunclebill
content: unless you trigger the vc to go into this mode only during non-finality i guess

author: nflaig
content: we would get rid of duties tracking on vc and always send all validator indices, but that also means you can't send out ptc attestations eagerly, you always have to wait until the deadline but that seems simpler than tracking duties for different branches, we don't even have a mechanism right now that would work to handle reorgs of duties due to dependent root changes on non-canonical branches

author: 0xunclebill
content: i think you you could still send out eagerly, you'd just need to do another round at the deadline

author: nflaig
content: we could do that, but that means several calls per slot and then beacon node needs to be aware of the deadline and only create attestations for present/available payloads before the deadline
